test_bit_flip_invalidates_signature().details: This test is intended to run with REAL test vectors from gen_test_vectors.rs.      The TODO below should be replaced with actual vector data.

test_gas_cost_measurement().details: Expected: approximately 3,450 gas per call (§1 of spec).

test_nist_p256_valid_signature().details: These test vectors are from https://csrc.nist.gov/CSRC/media/Projects/      Cryptographic-Algorithm-Validation-Program/documents/dss/186-3ecdsatestvectors.zip      (P-256, SHA-256, test case 1).      IMPORTANT: The actual test vectors will be populated by running:        cargo run --manifest-path scripts/Cargo.toml --bin gen_test_vectors      The Rust p256 crate generates deterministic RFC 6979 signatures.      These placeholder vectors test the test framework; real vectors go in      the production test suite.

failed().notice: Returns true if any test assertion has failed.

targetSenders().notice: Returns sender allowlist configured via `targetSender` (empty means no sender allowlist).

test_bit_flip_invalidates_signature().notice: Bit flip test: changing one bit in the signature should invalidate it.

test_gas_cost_measurement().notice: Measure gas cost of the precompile and confirm it's in the expected range.

test_nist_p256_valid_signature().notice: Test a valid P-256 signature from the NIST CAVS test suite.

test_precompile_does_not_revert_on_bad_i.notice: Confirm the precompile does not revert even on completely invalid input.
